target.theorem: cechCofaceMap_pi_smul (per-summand hG discharge at L1147, was L988)

findings.blocker: eqToHom-vs-Pi.π transport identification at coordinate j' — Mathlib does not expose eqToHom-Pi.π transport for object-equality eqToHom (only `Pi.π_comp_eqToHom` for index equality). The wrapper `cechCofaceMap_summand_family'` was defined as a direct Pi.lift (not as `named_family ≫ eqToHom`) precisely to escape the discrim-tree blocker, but this defers the per-coordinate equality to the proof of L1147 — the body of L1147 must now bridge `F_at_i ≫ eqToHom_outer` vs `wrapper` at the per-coord level.

attempts.strategy: Define Helper 1 — wrapper def `cechCofaceMap_summand_family'` as a direct `Pi.lift` over Fin (n+1) with `Fin.cast` on inner indices.
attempts.code_tried:
noncomputable def cechCofaceMap_summand_family' ... :=
  have hRel : (ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 2 = n + 1 := by rcases n with _ | k; · omega; · simp [ComplexShape.prev, ComplexShape.up_Rel]
  Pi.lift fun j_new => Pi.π (...) ((j_new ∘ Fin.cast hRel) ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ (Fin.cast hRel.symm i)).toOrderHom) ≫ (toModuleKPresheaf C).map (Pi.lift fun x => Pi.π (...) (Fin.cast hRel ((SimplexCategory.δ (Fin.cast hRel.symm i)).toOrderHom x))).op
attempts.insight: Defining the wrapper as a *direct* Pi.lift (rather than `named_family ≫ eqToHom`) sidesteps the discrim-tree blocker for the binder-level R-linearity proof — at the cost of moving the eqToHom-vs-Pi.π transport burden into the L1147 client proof.

attempts.strategy: Define Helper 2 — `cechCofaceMap_summand_family'_R_linear` (R-linearity transport), mirroring iter-104's binder-level proof of `cechCofaceMap_summand_family_R_linear` with Fin (n+1) outer index and Fin.cast on the inner δ-composition.
attempts.code_tried:
theorem cechCofaceMap_summand_family'_R_linear hU s₀ n hn i := by
  intro R Z₁ Z₂ e₁ e₂
  have hRel : ... := by rcases n with _ | k; · omega; · simp [ComplexShape.prev, ComplexShape.up_Rel]
  letI perI₁ ... letI h_mod_pi₁ ... letI perI₂ ... letI h_mod_pi₂ ...
  intro r y; funext j_new
  simp only [Pi.smul_apply]
  show (Pi.π Z₂ j_new).hom (...) = r • (Pi.π Z₂ j_new).hom (...)
  unfold cechCofaceMap_summand_family'
  simp only [Limits.Pi.lift_π_apply, ConcreteCategory.comp_apply]
  have hSym ... := ModuleCat.piIsoPi_inv_kernel_ι_apply Z₁ _ _
  rw [hSym, hSym]
  simp only [Pi.smul_apply, RingHom.toModule_smul]
  set Pl := Pi.lift fun x => Pi.π _ (Fin.cast hRel (δ x)) with hPl_def
  exact ((C.left.presheaf.map Pl.op).hom.map_mul _ _).trans
    (congrArg (· * (C.left.presheaf.map Pl.op).hom (y ((j_new ∘ Fin.cast hRel) ∘ δ.toOrderHom)))
      (presheafMap_restrict_collapse _ _ _ r))
attempts.goal_before: ∀ (r : R) (y : ∀ i, Z₁ i), e₂ ((cechCofaceMap_summand_family' s₀ n hn i).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) = r • e₂ ((cechCofaceMap_summand_family' s₀ n hn i).hom (e₁.symm y))
attempts.insight: The wrapper's R-linearity proof reuses the iter-104 binder-level pattern verbatim — RingHom.toModule_smul + piIsoPi_inv_kernel_ι_apply + term-level Eq.trans + congrArg + presheafMap_restrict_collapse. The Fin.cast index translation cleanly factors out and presheafMap_restrict_collapse still fires (the morphism Pl is structurally the same as iter-104's, just with Fin.cast-translated indices).

attempts.strategy: Apply wrapper R-linearity directly + congrFun + `simp only [Pi.smul_apply]` to get h_wrap_pt; then write structured partial proof with `sorry` for the residual eqToHom-vs-Pi.π transport identification.
attempts.code_tried:

have h_wrap := cechCofaceMap_summand_family'_R_linear hU s₀ n hn (Fin.cast hRel' i)
have h_wrap_pt := congrFun (h_wrap r' y') j'
simp only [Pi.smul_apply] at h_wrap_pt
sorry
attempts.goal_after: case h.h with h_wrap_pt : (Pi.π Z₂ j').hom (wrapper.hom (e₁.symm (r' • y'))) = r' • (Pi.π Z₂ j').hom (wrapper.hom (e₁.symm y')) in context
attempts.insight: The wrapper R-linearity equation `h_wrap_pt` provides exactly the desired form modulo the eqToHom-vs-Pi.π transport identification: goal LHS has `(Pi.π Z₂ j').hom (eqToHom.hom (((-1)^↑i • F_at_i).hom z))` while h_wrap_pt has `(Pi.π Z₂ j').hom (wrapper.hom z)`. Reducing one to the other requires (a) pulling `(-1)^↑i •` out (smul_comm + map_zsmul), (b) identifying `F_at_i ≫ eqToHom_outer = wrapper at (Fin.cast hRel' i)` per-coord. Step (b) is morphism-level equality on Pi.lifts via Pi.lift_ext + Fin.cast_cast round-trip.

attempts.strategy: Probe `rw [ModuleCat.piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype_apply, ModuleCat.piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype_apply] at h_wrap_pt` to convert h_wrap_pt's `e₂ x j'` form into `(Pi.π Z₂ j').hom x` form, then try further rewriting.

attempts.insight: Forward progress committed: wrapper infrastructure (Helper 1 + Helper 2) is in place and proved, fully closing both bodies. The L1147 partial proof documents the precise structural gap (eqToHom-vs-Pi.π transport at per-coord level) and primes iter-104's three concrete routes: morphism-equality lemma via Pi.lift_ext, rcases-on-n collapsing eqToHom, or `convert h_wrap_pt using 3` + per-coord transport sub-goals.

next_steps: iter-106 plan should commit to one of three concrete routes per the prover's recommendations: (Route 1) add a top-level morphism-equality lemma `cechCofaceMap_summand_family_comp_eqToHom_eq : F_at_i ≫ eqToHom_outer = cechCofaceMap_summand_family' s₀ n hn (Fin.cast hRel' i)`, proved by `Limits.Pi.hom_ext`/`limit_ext` + per-coord `Pi.lift_π_apply` + `Fin.cast_cast`; (Route 2) rcases-on-n + simp [ComplexShape.prev, ComplexShape.up_Rel] BEFORE the wrapper invocation so eqToHom dissolves into refl-identity; (Route 3) `convert h_wrap_pt using 3` to expose the eqToHom-per-coord sub-goals directly. Route 1 is the cleanest morphism-level move; Route 3 is the most direct from the current state. NB: with the wrapper helpers now durably committed and proved, the residual is **morphism-level**, not the discrim-tree class that defeated iter-099/100/101/103.
